preamble: The transformation of the nasalized open front vowel [ã] into the diphthong [uo] is a form of diphthongization, often involving complex breaking and shifting of the vowel quality and nasalization.

examples.note: Portuguese exhibits complex breaking and development of nasal vowels into various diphthongs and vowel combinations.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
